info.name: IAM Server API
info.description: Comprehensive API test collection for the IAM Server. 55+ tests across 10 folders covering all endpoints.

exec: pm.test('DB component UP', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.status).to.eql('UP'); pm.expect(j.components.db.status).to.eql('UP'); });

pm.test('Has accessToken', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.accessToken).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.collectionVariables.set('accessToken', j.accessToken); pm.collectionVariables.set('refreshToken', j.refreshToken); if (j.user && j.user.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('adminUserId', j.user.id.toString()); });
pm.test('User has id+username', () => { const u = pm.response.json().user; pm.expect(u).to.have.property('id'); pm.expect(u).to.have.property('username'); });

item.name: POST /auth/login -- 401 wrong password no user enumeration

exec: pm.test('401 not 404 no user enumeration', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

pm.test('New accessToken', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.accessToken).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.collectionVariables.set('accessToken', j.accessToken); pm.collectionVariables.set('refreshToken', j.refreshToken); });

pm.test('Paginated content', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.content).to.be.an('array'); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('totalElements'); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('totalPages'); });

pm.test('User created', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.username).to.eql('testuser_newman'); pm.expect(j.email).to.eql('testuser_newman@example.com'); pm.expect(j.enabled).to.be.true; pm.collectionVariables.set('testUserId', j.id.toString()); });
// Handle 409 Conflict: resource exists from a previous run — look up its ID
if (err || res.code !== 200) { console.error('Lookup failed:', err); return; }
pm.test('Role created', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.name).to.eql('ROLE_NEWMAN_TEST'); pm.collectionVariables.set('testRoleId', j.id.toString()); });
// Handle 409 Conflict: resource exists from a previous run — look up its ID
if (err || res.code !== 200) { console.error('Lookup failed:', err); return; }
pm.test('Permission created', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.name).to.eql('NEWMAN_TEST_PERM'); pm.collectionVariables.set('testPermId', j.id.toString()); });
// Handle 409 Conflict: resource exists from a previous run — look up its ID
if (err || res.code !== 200) { console.error('Lookup failed:', err); return; }

pm.test('Session fields present', () => { const s = pm.response.json(); if (s.length > 0) { pm.expect(s[0]).to.have.property('sessionId'); pm.expect(s[0]).to.have.property('ipAddress'); pm.expect(s[0]).to.have.property('createdAt'); pm.expect(s[0]).to.have.property('active'); } });

pm.test('Has all policy fields', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('minLength'); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('requireUppercase'); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('requireLowercase'); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('requireNumbers'); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('requireSpecialChars'); });
pm.test('Status 400 for wrong old password', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));
pm.test('Paginated content', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.content).to.be.an('array'); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('totalElements'); });

pm.test('Audit entries have required fields', () => { const logs = pm.response.json().content; if (logs.length > 0) { pm.expect(logs[0]).to.have.property('action'); pm.expect(logs[0]).to.have.property('username'); pm.expect(logs[0]).to.have.property('timestamp'); } });
console.log('Skipping cleanup: testPermId is not set');
console.log('Skipping cleanup: testRoleId is not set');
console.log('Skipping cleanup: testUserId is not set');
console.log('Skipping cleanup: testUserId is not set');
